The JsonParser is used with default settings that enable class metadata deserialization via __class field, allowing attacker-controlled JSON to instantiate arbitrary classes and potentially execute malicious code.

claude-opus-4-5: 0
The JsonParser is instantiated with default settings and ClassMetadataName is not set, so there is no deserialization vulnerability in this code.

gemini-2.5-pro-06-17: 0
The code uses the default configuration of jodd.json.JsonParser without setting ClassMetadataName, which prevents the polymorphic deserialization vulnerability.

fix.securityReason:
qwen3-235b-a22b-thinking-2507: 0
The code uses JsonParser.create() without setting ClassMetadataName, so it does not enable unsafe deserialization features that could lead to arbitrary class instantiation.

claude-opus-4-5: 0
The code uses JsonParser.create().parse() without setting ClassMetadataName, which means type metadata processing is not enabled and there is no deserialization vulnerability.

gemini-2.5-pro-06-17: 0
The code uses jodd.json.JsonParser with its default settings and does not set ClassMetadataName, thus avoiding the remote code execution vulnerability associated with deserializing arbitrary classes.
